title: refactor: move tier progression flow into enter app
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Tidying the garden shed
gist.blurb: We've reorganized the internal logic that handles tier upgrades, moving the Spore-to-Seed flow into its proper home in the Enter app. Just some spring cleaning to keep the codebase healthy!
gist.summary: Refactored the tier progression system by moving the Spore-to-Seed upgrade flow into the enter.pollinations.ai application and extracting shared helpers.
gist.impact: This internal reorganization makes the tier upgrade logic easier to maintain and extends our shared infrastructure.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot organizing boxes or files in a cozy garden shed. A robot helper holding a 'Spore to Seed' folder. Represents internal refactoring and moving logic to its proper place.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, organizing glowing lime green (#ecf874) folders in a rustic garden shed. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds a folder labeled 'SEED'. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, organizing glowing lime green (#ecf874) folders in a rustic garden shed.
